question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail his plans to provide the sheep sector with financial support in the proposed new measures.
answer_text: The Sheep Industry Taskforce produced its report to identify the needs of the sheep sector in Northern Ireland and a proposed package to encourage its development.My officials are currently working to better understand the strategic needs of this sector and I will consider deploying levers to meet those needs.  It is too early in the policy development process to give any indication of what might be included in any sheep scheme moving forward.  Officials recently met the Sheep Industry Taskforce to discuss the report and agree next steps.  I look forward to receiving an update on these discussions.  Any decision to introduce new policies or programmes will be in the context of budget availability within the ear-marked farm support funding envelope.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to outline their future plans for the development of the former prison buildings at the Maze/Long Kesh site.

To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he will engage with the Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs to ensure that the proposed fees on Extended Producer Responsibility do not disadvantage glass as a more recyclable material than others, such as plastic.
answer_text: I acknowledge the concerns around indicative costs for glass producers and can assure you that these have been relayed to DEFRA.We have heard and understand the points raised by the glass industry in relation to the illustrative base fees. We recently held a meeting with the glass industry to discuss the challenges and will continue to review the approach in response to industry feedback.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the business case for the Northern Ireland Football Fund.

To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, in relation to the Agri-Food and BioSciences Institute research study that found ammonia emissions from some poultry units are significantly lower than the estimates previously used by the Northern Ireland Environment Agency, to provide an update on assessments to planning applications in relation to ammonia measurements.
answer_text: Question 13 - Tom ElliotTo ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, in relation to the Agri-Food and BioSciences Institute research study that found ammonia emissions from some poultry units are significantly lower than the estimates previously used by the Northern Ireland Environment Agency, to provide an update on assessments to planning applications in relation to ammonia measurements.AQO 763/22-27Answer:In common with all the UK environment agencies,  NIEA uses emissions factors agreed upon for use in compiling the UK National Emissions Inventory. Where additional research supports new or revised emission factors, NIEA endeavours to update the assessment procedures as soon as the evidence can be verified.   NIEA received the AFBI research report on the updated broiler emissions factor in February 2022, reflecting current genetics, feed efficiency, heating system and management practices.  After reviewing the findings, it was subsequently incorporated into NIEA’s assessment procedures for appropriate planning applications in a matter of weeks.  An updated planning advice note detailing the updated emission factor was published in March 2022.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he will reinstate an independent advisory body to ensure that his Department is fully equipped to address the ongoing burden of TB.
answer_text: The bovine Tuberculosis (bTB) Eradication Partnership (TBEP) was established in June 2018 to provide independent expert advice on the development and implementation of a new bTB Eradication Strategy.  Following a number of extensions, the tenure of the most recent members of the TBEP expired on 31 December 2023. My Department commissioned an independent strategic review of the TBEP under the auspices of the Strategic Investment Board (SIB). A draft report has been received from the SIB and is now nearly finalised, it will help inform decisions on the way forward.I have also asked my Chief Veterinary Officer (CVO) to review and examine all matters relating to the bTB programme and policy, including the actions contained in the bTB Eradication Strategy and our approach to stakeholder engagement.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he will publish the report from the assessment that his Department commissioned prior to the conclusion of the TB Eradication Partnership by an external body.
answer_text: My Department commissioned an independent strategic review of the TB Eradication Partnership (TBEP) under the auspices of the Strategic Investment Board (SIB). A draft Report has been received from the SIB. Once this is finalised it will be shared with former members of the partnership and consideration will be given on whether it will be more widely published.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, in relation to cases where XL Bully dog owners have not applied for an exemption certificate before 1 January 2025 and they must surrender their XL Bully dog, to detail what happens to the dog.
answer_text: Beyond 1 January 2025 it will be illegal to own an XL Bully dog without an exemption certificate.  Therefore, if a dog is identified as an XL Bully by a dog warden after 1 January 2025 without an exemption certificate, it will be seized and the owner could be convicted of an offence.  A court could order a dog seized as a restricted type to be euthanised, even where the council decides not to prosecute the owner. If the court is persuaded that the dog will not be a danger to the public, the court may make a ‘contingent destruction order’ that exempts the dog from the ban for so long as the exemption conditions are met. In these circumstances an owner has two months to demonstrate that the exemption conditions have been met. If the exemption conditions are met, the council must issue a certificate of exemption and a licence for that dog. However, if the exemption conditions are not met, the dog will be euthanised.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail what happens to XL Bully dogs after 5 July 2024 in cases where the owner has passed away if transfer of ownership is not permitted.
answer_text: My Department intends to shortly bring forward legislation to enact an exemption scheme for those owners who wish to keep their XL Bully dogs.It is planned that, in the situation where the owner dies, the exemption process will allow three months for any person inheriting the dog to apply for a new certificate of exemption. As long as councils deem the transfer legitimate, are satisfied with evidence provided and the new owner is 16 years or older and therefore eligible to seek exemption, transfer of ownership will be permitted.  Alternatively, the person who has inherited the dog will have 3-month window to have the dog euthanised. Where the local council refuses the application legal ownership of an XL Bully dog ceases 14 days after the determination. This will allow a small period of time for arrangements to be made to euthanise the dog whilst avoiding court proceedings and potential criminalisation.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for a breakdown of financial allocations to each Department from the Regional Rates collected in each of the last four years.
answer_text: The income from Regional Rates is not separately allocated to departments.Rather, it is combined with other Executive funds, including the Block Grant from Treasury, to determine the total sum available for allocation.Therefore, it is not possible to determine how much each department received from the Regional Rates

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether she will consider providing a rates break for vacant domestic properties.
answer_text: There are already a large number of exclusions from the charge for vacant rating, for example certain evacuated dwellings, listed buildings etc.  While I am always happy to consider additional exclusions, the current exclusions were carefully developed through detailed public consultation processes and guard against rates avoidance tactics. The current policy arose out of the 2007 Semple Review on Affordable Housing to help address housing shortages. In 2024 we once again find ourselves in the middle of a housing crisis that the new Executive is actively addressing. I have no plans to provide a rates break for vacant domestic properties.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on his plans for a new build for Enniskillen Royal Grammar School.
To ask the Minister of Justice how much has been paid in Legal Aid in each year of the last five years.
answer_text: AnswerLegal Aid expenditure in the last 5 years has been as follows:2023-24: £113.5m2022-23: £101.3m2021-22: £94.7m2020-21: £75.2m2019-20: £82.1m2023-24 figures are still subject to audit, however previous years are detailed in the Legal Services Agency’s Annual Report and Accounts.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of the Grant Thornton report on the financial impact of Northern Ireland hosting the 2028 UEFA European Football Championship, including whether it represents an independent and robust evidence-based consideration of the issues.
answer_text: The report adds to the evidence base on the significant benefits of co-hosting the EUROs, which is the third biggest sporting event in the world.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice what role her Department has in preparations for 2028 UEFA European Football Championship games to be played in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: My Department has no direct role in preparations for the 2028 UEFA European Football Championships.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the projected expenditure by her Department in relation to the 2028 UEFA European Football Championship.
answer_text: Currently my Department has no projected expenditure in relation to the 2028 UEFA European Football Championship.If there are associated policing costs, the allocation of policing resources is an operational matter for the Chief Constable, who is accountable to the Northern Ireland Policing Board. I am committed to respecting the operational independence of the Chief Constable and the role of the Policing Board. You may therefore wish to direct your question to the Police Service of Northern Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether the projected cost for policing 2028 UEFA European Football Championship matches in Northern Ireland will be met directly from her departmental budget or by His Majesty’s Government.
answer_text: The allocation of policing resources is an operational matter for the Chief Constable, who is accountable to the Northern Ireland Policing Board. I am committed to respecting the operational independence of the Chief Constable and the role of the Policing Board. You may therefore wish to direct your question to the Police Service of Northern Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the estimated cost for the redevelopment of Casement Park.
answer_text: As the procurement for a new construction contract for the redevelopment of Casement Park is a live issue, there are commercial sensitivities that mean I cannot provide an update on the estimated cost at this time.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to outline the intended scope of the future health service reimbursement scheme prior to the Executive's approval of the Budget 2024-25.
answer_text: The Reimbursement Scheme bid of £31million was part of a wider bid for £135million of additional funding to tackle waiting lists. The 2024/25 Budget recently approved by the Executive provides a missed opportunity to improve waiting lists. If the £135million had been invested as I had bid, above the £75-80million required for red-flag and time critical patients, significant progress could have been made. Following the success of the scheme I introduced in June 2021, I had hoped to introduce a new reimbursement scheme that could have treated over 4000 patients this financial year. Based on the previous reimbursement scheme whereby 88% of claims were for orthopaedic treatments, in this financial period, I would have expected a new scheme to have treated over 3500 patients who are waiting for orthopaedic treatment. This was in addition to a separate bid of £53million, within the wider £135million, specifically to address some 4,400 patients waiting over 2 years for orthopaedic procedures.It was intended that a new reimbursement scheme would have a wider span than the Republic of Ireland, however this was still being finalised as ensuring compliance with various international agreements post Brexit is more complex. Unfortunately, under the 2024/25 Budget, precisely zero additional funding has been provided for tackling our waiting lists. Instead of now being able to invest in such waiting list initiatives, my Department’s budget has been cut by over 2%. This is a deeply regrettable and concerning position.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) the number of unadopted housing developments in Fermanagh and South Tyrone; and (ii) the names of these developments.
answer_text: Following introduction of the Local Government Act in 2014 my Department stores information consistent with the district council areas.  Unfortunately, DfI Private Streets records are not stored in a fashion that can be extracted by electoral area. However, below is a list of the currently bonded phases of housing developments for the Dungannon and Fermanagh areas, I trust this is acceptable.Many developments are taken forward in phases, of question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he intends to bring forward a support package for farmers who host migrating birds such as geese and swans over the winter months, similar to what is available in the Republic of Ireland.
answer_text: My Department’s current Farm Support and Development Programme is planned to contain similar elements to the Agri-Climate Rural Environment Scheme (ACRES) that is being introduced in Ireland.  I am always keen to learn from others about interventions that can support both farmers and our rich biodiversity and have asked my officials to liaise with their Irish counterparts as this scheme progresses so that any key learning can be captured and applied as we move forward our farm support programmes.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the amount spent on repairs to the surface and structure of the A4 and A5 Ballygawley roundabout in the last five years.
answer_text: During the last 5 years routine inspections have recorded 11 no. potholes for repair, 6 no. repairs to Chevrons and Signs, 3 no. areas requiring sweeping and 1 no. gully lid that needed to be reset. These repairs have all been carried out by our direct labour workforce. My Department do not keep records of individual repair costs completed by our direct labour workforce.Over the same period a 3 no. repairs have been carried out on the Vehicle Restraint System (crash barrier) in the vicinity of the roundabout. This specialist work was undertaken by external contractors at a total cost of £22,003.43.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to outline any discussions that their representatives in Brussels have had with EU officials in relation to the provisions in the Windsor Framework relating to veterinary medicines.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what plans are in place to clear debris from the River Blackwater.
answer_text: Under the terms of the Drainage (NI) Order 1973, my Department’s River Directorate inspects and maintains the River Blackwater from a flood risk perspective. To alleviate the impacts of flooding my Department will continue its inspection and maintenance regime on the River Blackwater, which will include the removal of material that could cause a significant drainage impediment. If debris does not interfere with the efficiency of the watercourse, we are not compelled to take action.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on plans for Lisnaskea Health and Care Centre.
answer_text: Design work on the Lisnaskea Health and Care Centre project has been completed and the construction works have been tendered.  The tender closed on 19th March 2024. The returned tenders are currently being evaluated.Construction work is scheduled to commence on site in Summer 2024, subject to successful procurement outcomes and Full Business Case approval.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether his Department provided equipment and insurance cover to local volunteers for the recent clean up of the River Blackwater.
answer_text: I can confirm that my Department did not request or provide any assistance for these works undertaken by other stakeholders. From a managing flood risk perspective, my Department’s Rivers Directorate will continue its cyclical inspection and maintenance regime on the River Blackwater.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the average timeframe for a reasonable adjustments application to be completed upon request in the NI Civil Service.
answer_text: The Civil Service considers all requests for reasonable adjustments in accordance with its obligations under the Disability Discrimination Act 1995 and is committed to creating and sustaining an inclusive workplace. Requests for reasonable adjustments are dealt with on a case-by-case basis.  There is no prescribed timeframe for the completion of reasonable adjustment requests as this will depend on the nature of the adjustment being requested.Data on the timeframe for completion of a reasonable adjustment request is not collected by the Civil Service.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance how many workplace discrimination cases have been brought against the NI Civil Service in the last 10 years.
answer_text: ANSWERHR functions for the Civil Service were centralised in the Department of Finance in 2017.  Prior to this, individual Departments managed their own HR functions.  Accordingly, there may be case records held by other Departments that predate the transfer of the function in 2017.Based on records available in my Department, there have been 42,522 Industrial Tribunal claims for various types of discrimination lodged against Civil Service Departments since 2014.  Of this figure, 41,489 (98%) relate to a single class action for injury to feelings claims based on age discrimination which were brought by NIPSA in respect of public sector pension schemes.  While the cases have been brought against Civil Service Departments, they do not all relate to employment in the Civil Service.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to outline what procedures are in place to deal with and support individuals who experience workplace bullying in the NI Civil Service.
answer_text: I refer you to my recent response to AQW 6914/22-27, which outlines the procedures in place to deal with and support individuals who experience workplace bullying in the Civil Service.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to outline whether they have a policy on the provision of bereavement payments to relatives of people killed during the Troubles.
answer_text: Our department has been funding the Victims and Survivors Service to provide financial support to bereaved victims and survivors for many years.  We are pleased that in April 2021 we were able to reopen the scheme for new clients, so that bereaved victims can avail of this support. This financial year, payments totalling over £2.2 million have been paid to 3,688 bereaved individuals, 1,034 of whom were new clients.   In addition, all victims and survivors have full access to the frameworks of services and support.We have received the Commissioner’s recent advice paper on a scheme for bereaved victims. We will consider this paper, and the wider needs of bereaved victims and survivors, as the new Strategy for Victims and Survivors is developed.							We are extremely sympathetic to the issues affecting bereaved victims and survivors, and we are keen to address their needs and acknowledge the ongoing loss felt by many.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to provide an update on the elective surgery that is being delivered at South West Acute Hospital.
answer_text: The South West Acute Hospital provides elective sessions for General Surgery, Paediatrics, Gynaecology, Dental, Breast and Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) with over 2800 patients treated since April 2022. The development of the Elective Overnight Stay Centre at the South West Acute Hospital has seen patients treated from across Northern Ireland and surgeons travelling to operate at the site.  Elective Overnight Stay Centres will enhance the quality and consistency of care, improve productivity, and will play a vital role in our efforts to reduce waiting lists.Like all hospitals providing elective care, the South West Acute Hospital has been working to rebuild to commissioned capacity following the pandemic.  As of January 2024, the South West Acute Hospital is delivering 17 out of 19.5 sessions per week with the rebuild to full capacity expected in March 2024.Plans for further expansion will be considered in this context and will be subject to the sustainable funding/workforce required to build capacity.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department intends to raise the level of student (i) maintenance grant; and (ii) loan for the incoming 2024-25 academic year.
answer_text: The higher education student support package for academic year 2024/25 was  determined before I took up ministerial office.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for an update on the proposed consultation for the Consolidation of Waste Recycling in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: A draft consultation titled ‘Rethinking Our Resources: Measures for Climate Action and a Circular Economy in NI’ has been prepared by my officials, setting out several options for household and non-household waste management in Northern Ireland. This is at the final stages of the review process.  Once approved for publication, a consultation will be launched seeking views from all stakeholders on proposals to help meet our new targets.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department will commit to dualling and upgrading the A4 Enniskillen to Ballygawley road.
answer_text: The Department is in the process of developing a new Transport Strategy that will help to shape the vision and strategic priorities for transport through to 2035.  Once this is published, it will help to inform the Regional Strategic Transport Network Transport Plan.  The A4 is part of the Strategic Road Network and will be considered in this Plan.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) how much finance; and (ii) how many projects Invest NI has delivered to the Fermanagh and South Tyrone constituency, in each of the last five years.
answer_text: Invest NI reports its performance on a financial year basis with 2022-23, ending 31st March 2023, being the most recent year available.  Information relating to 2023-24 is provisionally planned for release in June 2024.  The table below details the number of projects (ii) and the amount of financial assistance (i) that Invest NI has offered towards these projects in the Fermanagh and South Tyrone Parliamentary Constituency between 2018-19 and 2022-23. The amount of total planned investment that this support has secured for the area is also included in the table.  Total planned investment is the combination of business investment and assistance offered by Invest NI in support of a project.In addition to financial assistance, Invest NI also provides wider support to all businesses through advice, assistance or guidance from our staff and through nibusinessinfo.co.uk, our online business advice portal.	Invest NI Number of Projects, Financial Assistance and Total Planned Investment in Fermanagh and South Tyrone Parliamentary Constituency (2018-19 to 2022-23)	YearNo of ProjectsTotal Invest NI Assistance £mTotal Planned Investment £m2018-191813.2915.682019-201874.9520.462020-211603.6922.132021-221874.8220.332022-231043.9517.59Total81920.6996.20

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to outline what progress has been made on Civil Service reform, as recommended by the Report of the Independent Public Inquiry into the Non-domestic Renewable Heat Incentive Scheme.
answer_text: Significant work has been done on reforms recommended by the Renewable Heat Incentive Inquiry. Action has been taken and tangible outputs can be demonstrated. The lessons from the Inquiry, along with the recommendations of the Public Accounts Committee and other external reviews into the Civil Service, have formed the basis for a longer-term body of work to ensure the service is able to fulfil its functions to the highest standard.  A sub-group of the NICS board has been established to put in place a one-year (2024/2025) people plan and to lead the development of a 2025-2030 People and Transformation Strategy.  This five-year strategy will align with the Programme for Government and support the people, organisational, leadership and change priorities for the future in a focused and coordinated way.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (i) for an update on the invasive zebra mussels at Movanagher Fish Farm; (ii) what impact the zebra mussels have had on the operations of Movanagher Fish Farm; and (iii) whether any fish have been moved from Movanagher Fish Farm since the discovery of zebra mussels on site.
answer_text: Zebra mussels are still present at Movanagher Fish Farm. My Department is working with the Agri-Food and Biosciences Institute (AFBI) to put in place a project to develop and assess zebra mussel treatment protocols for stocking that will comply with the Wildlife (Northern Ireland) Order 1985. The occurrence of zebra mussels at Movanagher Fish Farm has resulted in the ongoing suspension of stocking of fish from the site. The production of juvenile fish at the farm has also been suspended until stocking can comply with the Wildlife Order. In November 2023, 28,250 fish were culled for welfare reasons as they could not be stocked out from the farm.Three fish movements, with precautionary measures in place, took place within one month of the discovery of zebra mussels at Movanagher fish farm. However, further scientific and legal advice deemed that these measures would be inadequate for compliance under the Wildlife (Northern Ireland) Order 1985. No further movements have since been made.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for his Department's stocking plans for rainbow and brown trout in the Public Angling Estate in 2024.
answer_text: I cannot, at this time, provide details of plans to stock Public Angling Estate (PAE) waters in 2024, but I will be considering options for future stocking of the PAE in the coming weeks.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs when the movement of livestock from Great Britain to Northern Ireland can recommence following it being stopped due to bluetongue.
answer_text: Movements of ruminants from Great Britain to Northern Ireland can recommence when Great Britain recovers disease free status for Bluetongue.  The minimum period required before disease free status can be granted is two years from the date of the last positive case.		Movements may be permitted sooner should regionalisation for Bluetongue be pursued by the Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs and the devolved administrations with approval granted by the European Union.  This would then permit the movement of ruminants from areas outside control zones.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for an update on standstill rules for livestock movement from Great Britain to Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The movement of livestock from Great Britain (GB) to Northern Ireland (NI) is subject to The Trade in Animals and Related Products Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2011.In respect of livestock standstill requirements, under these regulations the movement of cattle, sheep, goats and pigs is subject to a standstill period on their holding of destination in NI for a period of at least 30 days following their movement from GB, unless consigned direct from the holding to a slaughterhouse. Under these regulations, livestock must also comply with a residency standstill period in GB as contained within the relevant Export Health Certificate conditions and in line with requirements under Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2020/692.  The length of the residency period differs depending on the species and the purpose of the movement, e.g. the GB residency period for bovine, ovine, caprine and porcine species intended for breeding and productions is 6 months, but the same species intended for slaughter have a 3-month GB residency requirement.In addition to the country residency period prior to movement to NI, there is also a residency requirement at establishment of origin level in GB.  The residency period at establishment of origin in GB is 30 days, or since birth, for movement of bovine, ovine, caprine and porcine species to NI.There are no GB country or establishment residency requirement for Northern Ireland origin cattle and sheep returning within 15 days from an approved show or sale in GB.There have been no recent changes or updates to these legislative standstill requirements.Please note, due to the current outbreak of Bluetongue Virus strain BTV-3 in GB, movement of all live ruminants from GB to NI is suspended.  No update is available yet as to when this trade will be able to resume, however, trade is not expected to resume in the near future.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, given that NIEA has adopted a new operational protocol for assessing ammonia impacts from planning applications, to detail (i) how his Department worked out NI site-relevant thresholds; and (ii) whether his Department make these sites publicly available for impacted ammonia sites.
answer_text: I am conscious that, informed by the requirements of the legislation in place to cover decision-making in the absence of ministers, my officials took a decision that they could no longer rely on published Ammonia Standing Advice, also known as the Operational Protocol, as the basis for statutory advice on planning applications. This decision followed very careful consideration of the legislative obligations placed on my Department.My focus is on developing a new ammonia strategy which will be accompanied by new advice to inform planning decisions.  I am determined that the new strategy and standing advice will be evidence-based and capable of ensuring full compliance with environmental law.  In the interim, NIEA has reverted to providing case-by-case advice on potential air pollution impacts from planning proposals.  This advice makes use of site-relevant thresholds that are determined for each individual application, that take account of the characteristics and specific environmental conditions at a site, as well as development pressure in proximity to the proposed project.  This approach follows guidance developed by the UK Joint Nature Conservation Committee (JNCC) to inform the assessment of air quality impacts on designated sites, on behalf of the Inter-agency Air Pollution Group and Defra.  ‘Guidance on Decision-making Thresholds for Air Pollution’ https://hub.jncc.gov.uk/assets/6cce4f2e-e481-4ec2-b369-2b4026c88447
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether the cost of implementing the Climate Change Act has been factored into the Department’s budget.
answer_text: My department is leading the development of Northern Ireland’s first Climate Action Plan, in collaboration with all departments. This Plan will set out Northern Ireland’s approach to delivering the emissions reductions required to achieve our first carbon budget. I intend to seek agreement from Executive colleagues for Northern Ireland’s first three carbon budgets. I will then bring forward draft regulations to the Assembly for its consideration and approval.I am fully committed to delivering a Climate Action Plan that is transformational, evidence-based and informed by stakeholders. The draft Climate Action Plan will need to be agreed by the Executive. The timeframes involved mean that it will not be possible to meet the dates for final publication as set out in the Climate Change Act. However I am determined that we will publish the draft for consultation as soon as possible.The Climate Action Plan will be considered by the DAERA Assembly Committee, and other Committees may also wish to scrutinise their departments’ contributions.As the member will be aware, budgets for the next financial year have not yet been confirmed, however the cost of implementing the Climate Change Act is being considered as part of the ongoing budget exercise for next year.  Work is ongoing to scope and refine these costs. Some of these will fall to DAERA but other departments will bear costs too. Normal budgeting processes will apply and it is also important that the costs and opportunities of addressing climate change are embedded within the economic appraisal processes. Finally, I want to emphasise that these up front costs represent investments in an improved society for future generations and that the cost of delaying action will ultimately be much greater. We must act now and we must act decisively.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (i) when he anticipates Zone 2 of the Soil Health Nutrient Scheme to be completed; and (ii) the reasons for the delay.
answer_text: I anticipate that sampling and analysis, for the Zone 2 farm businesses that registered for the Soil Nutrient Health Scheme, will be substantially complete by April 2024. The remaining fields will be completed during winter 2024-25.Soil sample collection takes place during the winter months and some poor weather events have caused a delay in parts of the 2023-24 programme. Therefore those fields which have not yet been sampled, and which have had nutrients applied after the end of the closed period for slurry spreading, i.e. 1 February onwards, will be rescheduled for winter 2024-25.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) whether he has considered bringing forward an Industry Supercharger Network Charging Compensation Scheme, similar to that in GB (ii) whether the compensation rates will be comparable to that of the GB scheme; and (iii) to provide a timeframe for delivering a similar scheme.
answer_text: My department is currently investigating the need and feasibility for a similar scheme to be introduced here.  If it is appropriate, a public consultation will be held to gather views from relevant stakeholders and the public to enhance the policy development and decision-making process.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what protection is in place for home owners and residents where a housing development is not complete and the developer is dissolved.
answer_text: Prior to construction, a developer is required to enter into Agreements with the Department and NI Water to provide the roads, footways and sewers to the standards prescribed in The Private Streets (Construction) Regulations (NI) 1994 to allow adoption.These agreements are secured by a bond, that may be used by DfI and/or NI Water to complete the road works or sewers should the developer default or be dissolved.    It is an offence to commence building works without making prior provision for street works costs.Where the bond is not sufficient to bring the development up to an adoptable standard and the developer has ceased trading, DfI and NI Water have no funding mechanism or legal responsibility to bridge the shortfall between the bond and the cost of the necessary works.  In these circumstances the development remains unadopted and responsibility for the site reverts to the frontagers/homeowners.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether there is a legal precedent regarding the liability to adopt a housing development, whereby its developer dissolved and the developer Bond is held.
answer_text: The Department is not obliged under The Private Streets (Northern Ireland) Order 1980 to serve  notice or carry out any enforcement actions necessary to bring a development up to an adoptable standard, whether the developer is trading or dissolved. The powers to serve notice and undertake works are discretionary.  NI Water has similar discretionary powers in the Water and Sewerage Services (Northern Ireland) Order 2006.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether his Department plans to bring forward a sheep support package that was not included in Future Agricultural Policy Decisions for Northern Ireland.
answer_text: In January 2023, the Sheep Industry Taskforce produced its report to identify the needs of the sheep sector in Northern Ireland and a proposed package to encourage its development.  I look forward to receiving a briefing on this report to better understand the strategic needs of the sector and will consider deploying levers to meet those needs. Any decision to introduce new policies or programmes will be in the context of budget availability within the ring-fenced farm support funding envelope.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to (i) provide an update on the A4 Enniskillen Southern Bypass scheme and; (ii) confirm whether his Department is committed to delivering this scheme without further delay.
answer_text: Major Road schemes are an important part of the work that is delivered by my Department as we seek to reduce journey times, increase reliability and improve road safety.In August 2022, I committed to progress the development of contract documents for this strategically important scheme in preparation for future procurement. This work is now at an advanced stage of completion, and I am in discussion with my executive colleagues to establish funding to allow the procurement process to commence for the construction of the project.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail what bids his Department has made to the Levelling Up Fund.
answer_text: Unfortunately my officials were unable to gather together the information sought by the member in the short amount of time I had remaining in office.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail what bids his Department has made to the Levelling Up Fund.
answer_text: This question was asked in the previous mandate and to the previous Minister for the Economy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail what bids his Department has made to the Levelling Up Fund.
answer_text: In respect of the Levelling Up Fund DAERA has not submitted any bids. My Department will continue to engage in relation to this Fund, as well as all schemes which fall under the Levelling Up Agenda, through the Department of Finance led cross-departmental Future Policy and Finance Workstream.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail what bids her Department has made to the Levelling Up Fund.
answer_text: The Department has not made any bids to the UK Government’s Levelling Up Fund as Northern Ireland Executive Departments are not eligible to bid for projects under the culture and regeneration themes. Information on all Levelling Up funds has been circulated to key stakeholders include Arms-Length Bodies, District Councils and voluntary and community sector organisations.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what flooding alleviation plans does his Department have in place for the Edereny area of Fermanagh this coming winter.
answer_text: The work on storm water drainage systems and gullies infrastructure is an operational matter for NI Water, and therefore you should direct your query to NI Water at waterline@niwater.com.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what investment in flooding alleviation schemes for Fermanagh and South Tyrone has been delivered this year.
answer_text: My Department has no plans to implement any flood alleviation schemes in the Fermanagh and South Tyrone area this year.  However, there are plans to carry out upgrade works to a designated watercourse inlet structure in Irvinestown and to the Lock Gates at the Portora Sluice Gates in Enniskillen at a cost of £110k.In addition, my Department has spent £128.5k in carrying out routine watercourse maintenance and a further £147k in drainage infrastructure repairs in the Fermanagh and South Tyrone area this year to help reduce flood risk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what flooding alleviation plans his Department has in place for Cappy Mill Race, Lisbellaw this coming winter.
answer_text: My Department has no formal flood alleviation plans for the Cappy Mill Race River in Lisbellaw, County Fermanagh.  However, following a request from a resident concerned about flooding at their property, my officials have agreed to reduce the inspection and maintenance frequency on the Cappy Mill Race River from every 6 years to every 4 years to help reduce flood risk in the area. In addition, my Department will continue to carry out weekly inspections and clearance of the designated watercourse inlet structures within the Lisbellaw area.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what emergency plans his Department has in place for flooding this coming winter in the Fermanagh and South Tyrone, particularly the Fermanagh Lakelands.
answer_text: My Department operates an on-call duty and flooding officer rota system 24 hours a day, 365 days a year with staff on standby to respond to flooding.  Routine cyclical inspections also take place on culvert inlet screens and grilles and the maintenance of these is usually increased before and during significant periods of heavy rainfall.  Sandbag stores at operational depots, and those at more remote locations close to communities at known flood risk, are fully stocked in preparation for any potential flooding. In addition, as the threat for any potential flooding increases, sandbags can be placed on mobile units to enable a timely response as necessary.My officials have worked with partner organisations to develop a multi-agency severe weather plan. This plan details the pre-planned arrangements for a co-ordinated multi-agency response as a result of a warning for severe weather.My Department also works with multi-agency partners, including Local Government, response organisations and the voluntary sector, to provide support to local communities in Fermanagh and South Tyrone.  This work, delivered through the Regional Community Resilience Group, involves engagement with communities at risk of flooding to help them prepare for and respond to weather related emergencies, and to provide co-ordinated multi agency support to individual properties or communities that may be cut off by flood water and require access to essential services.  Through this community resilience work my Department seeks to provide an extra layer of support in addition to the normal response for those who may be impacted by flooding in periods of severe weather.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice (i) what steps are being taken to commission the Police Museum; (ii) whether the amount that was ring-fenced for this project is still available; (iii) how much funding was ring-fenced for the project; (iv) whether a site has been identified for the museum; and (v) has consideration been given to using the St Lucia Barracks in Omagh for this project.
answer_text: The Police Service of Northern Ireland (PSNI) is responsible for the Police Museum, including consideration of sites. The Chief Constable has advised that the outline business case will be completed for submission this month and subject to the necessary approvals, it is anticipated that the museum will be completed by late 2025 at Brooklyn, Knock Road, Belfast. If you require any further information with regards to questions (i), (iv), and (v), you may wish to contact the PSNI directly.  I can confirm that the £5.5m funding committed by Treasury for a new Police Museum is still available for drawdown. In terms of the £383k that relates to the unallocated amount from the Police Part Time Reserve Gratuity Scheme, I can also confirm that the then Minister of Justice, Minister Ford, decided that this funding would be made available to the new Police Museum. I remain committed that this funding will be prioritised.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the total spend on maintenance and security at the St Lucia barracks Omagh in the last ten years.
answer_text: The St Lucia site transferred to my Department in May 2016.  I am therefore only able to provide you with the financial information relating to spend by my Department from May 2016 to 30 September 2022.Total security costs for the site from  May 2016 – 30 September 2022 - £269,521.05Total maintenance costs for the site from May 2016 – 30 September 2022 - £123.699.79Total costs for both security and maintenance at the site from May 2016 – 30 September 2022 -  £393,220.84

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what the current status is of the Strule Shared Educational Campus in Omagh.
answer_text: The previous Main Works procurement competition has been closed and the Department formally commenced a fresh Main Works procurement competition with the release of the Invitation to Tender on 13 December 2021.On the current programme timetable, subject to all necessary approvals being in place, contract award is planned for spring 2023.  Assuming successful appointment of a main works contractor and following the pre-construction design period, construction is due to begin on site autumn 2023 and is currently planned to complete in 2026, with campus opening planned for September 2026.The delivery of all remaining construction projects is also progressing, including the Gortin Road/Mountjoy Road scheme which was approved by Fermanagh and Omagh District Council Planning Committee in February 2022. Work is continuing across all other projects and workstreams including detailing the work required to realise the educational benefits and pilot various shared education initiatives prior to go-live, the development of the arrangements for the ownership, governance, funding and management of the Campus and working with existing school site owners to consider how best to plan for and manage the future use and/or disposal of the existing sites following relocation.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether she will visit Omagh to see Omagh High School and Sacred Heart College, given that both schools need major investment to remain on their current site.
answer_text: I visited Sacred Heart College on 23 March 2022 and I plan to visit Omagh High School on 20 October 2022.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs what action he is taking to improve access to the Soil Nutrient Health Scheme.
answer_text: DAERA online services portal opened for registrations to the Soil Nutrient Health Scheme (SNHS) to Zone 1 farmers on 27 May 2022.  To register for SNHS a recognised farm business ID number is required along with a Government Gateway account access number.  The online portal registration includes the verification of fields applied for within a digitised map.  The Registration process is similar to completing the Single Application form.  However, to improve access for farmers and to facilitate those farmers who may have difficulty with the on-line process, my officials provided a demonstration on the use of the one line portal to an Association of farmers Agents, the Agri Consultants Association and to farming unions.  Staff at the DAERA Direct Offices also received training to support farmers’ registration.  In addition, the Agri-Food & Biosciences Institute (AFBI), who are the Delivery Agent for the Scheme, issued a step by step guide to all Zone 1 farmers on the use of the on-line registration portal along with an on-line video on ‘How to Register to the SNHS’.  CAFRE advisory staff have provided information to farmers on the benefits of joining the SNHS and guidance on accessing the on-line registration at various farming events, including Business Development Group meetings within Zone 1.  AFBI also provided contact points to facilitate any farmers who need assistance.  However, to date the vast majority have registered on-line without assistance.There are a number of options available for farmers to register to the SNHS.  Farmers holding a current Category 1 Farm Business ID number can choose to apply themselves through their own gateway account, or by authorising an agent to apply on their behalf, or by booking an appointment in their local DAERA Direct office and have a customer service staff member complete their registration online with them. The SNHS online registration period was due to close on 8 July 2022.  However, I recognise that this is a very busy period on farms and therefore, I have extended the registration period to 31 August 2022.  I would therefore encourage any farmers in Zone 1, who haven’t yet done so to register for the SNHS before the closing date of 31 August 2022.  The outputs will provide farmers with valuable baseline information on their soil nutrients, surface water run-off risk areas and farm carbon estimation. Importantly, farmers should be aware that participation in the SNHS is a condition of entry for future farm support schemes like the Farm Sustainability Payment, Farming with Nature and the Beef Sustainability Package. These schemes are likely to be important income streams for farm businesses.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs what options are available for farmers to register for the Soil Nutrient Health Scheme who do not have access to IT facilities or are not IT proficient.
answer_text: DAERA online services portal opened for registrations to the Soil Nutrient Health Scheme (SNHS) to Zone 1 farmers on 27 May 2022.  To register for SNHS a recognised farm business ID number is required along with a Government Gateway account access number.  The online portal registration includes the verification of fields applied for within a digitised map.  The Registration process is similar to completing the Single Application form.  However, to improve access for farmers and to facilitate those farmers who may have difficulty with the on-line process, my officials provided a demonstration on the use of the one line portal to an Association of farmers Agents, the Agri Consultants Association and to farming unions.  Staff at the DAERA Direct Offices also received training to support farmers’ registration.  In addition, the Agri-Food & Biosciences Institute (AFBI), who are the Delivery Agent for the Scheme, issued a step by step guide to all Zone 1 farmers on the use of the on-line registration portal along with an on-line video on ‘How to Register to the SNHS’.  CAFRE advisory staff have provided information to farmers on the benefits of joining the SNHS and guidance on accessing the on-line registration at various farming events, including Business Development Group meetings within Zone 1.  AFBI also provided contact points to facilitate any farmers who need assistance.  However, to date the vast majority have registered on-line without assistance.Therefore, there are a number of options available for farmers to register to the SNHS.  Farmers holding a current Category 1 Farm Business ID number can choose to apply themselves through their own gateway account, or by authorising an agent to apply on their behalf, or by booking an appointment in their local DAERA Direct office and have a customer service staff member complete their registration online with them. The SNHS online registration period was due to close on 8 July 2022.  However, I recognise that this is a very busy period on farms and therefore, I have extended the registration period to 31 August 2022.  I would therefore encourage any farmers in Zone 1, who haven’t yet done so to register for the SNHS before the closing date of 31 August 2022.  The outputs will provide farmers with valuable baseline information on their soil nutrients, surface water run-off risk areas and farm carbon estimation. Importantly, farmers should be aware that participation in the SNHS is a condition of entry for future farm support schemes like the Farm Sustainability Payment, Farming with Nature and the Beef Sustainability Package. These schemes are likely to be important income streams for farm businesses.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities (i) how many inspections of St Lucia Barracks, Omagh, has Historic Buildings Branch carried out since 2007; (ii) whether any comments were made, or concerns noted, in respect of maintenance of the buildings during these inspections; and (iii) whether remedial action was completed and subsequently reviewed by her Department.
answer_text: Architects from Historic Environment Division have carried out at least 14 visits in relation to buildings on the site since 2007. These visits were for a range of purposes including engagement on a site Masterplan and collation of photographic surveys of the listed buildings.  There have also been a number of visits by archaeologists from Historic Environment Division in relation to archaeological mitigation and discharge of conditions across the wider site. At a visit in Nov 2019 the need to address water ingress and ensure adequate levels of ventilation to allow the buildings to dry out was discussed.  Following this visit, seven of the listed structures on site were included on the Heritage at Risk Register.Engagement continues and officials met the heritage accredited surveyor and team onsite this year to establish and agree the scope and programming of remedial works, currently being undertaken. Officials have also engaged on a Listed Building Consent application for the work and continue to monitor progress of this work.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what is the average number of vehicle MOT tests carried out per month for (i) 2018; (ii) 2019; (iii) 2020; (iv) 2021; and (v) 2022, at each vehicle test centre.
answer_text: Official vehicle testing statistics are published on a monthly basis and can be accessed at https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/articles/driver-and-vehicle-agency-monthly-tests-conducted-statistics.  Table 1.3 includes details of full vehicle tests conducted at each test centre from 2014 onwards.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any discussions he has had with the Home Office and Northern Ireland's Passport Office in relation to the timescales for issuing passports to Northern Ireland residents.
answer_text: Given I have no responsibility for this issue whatsoever, I have not had any discussions with the Home Office, or the Passport Office, in relation to this issue.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) for an update on the Enniskillen Southern Bypass; and (ii) when he expects the construction to commence.
answer_text: I recognise that the Enniskillen Bypass scheme is of significant importance to the town of Enniskillen and the surrounding area.The decision to proceed and statutory orders for the scheme were made in 2021. Site clearance and fencing works commenced in January 2022, and are now substantially complete. NIE diversionary works are also scheduled to commence during the summer months.While I remain committed to such schemes, the refusal of the DUP to form an Executive means a three year budget cannot be agreed which makes it extremely difficult for my Department to plan future investment.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (i) what steps his Department is taking to reduce the use of antibiotics in farming; and (ii) to outline any decline in usage, in the last three years.
answer_text: In 2019, the Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (DAERA), the Department of Health (DoH) and the Food Standards Agency (FSA) launched the five year action plan titled ‘Changing the Culture - One Health’ action plan to fight antimicrobial resistance (AMR):Changing the Culture 2019-2024 – One Health | Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (daera-ni.gov.uk)DAERA’s specific actions within the plan include:Reducing the need for antimicrobials by lowering the burden of animal infection through implementation of strategies to reduce endemic disease (e.g. Bovine Viral Diarrhoea (BVD) eradication scheme) and the promotion of good farm biosecurity practices;Optimising the use of antimicrobials in animals and agriculture, for example, through the provision of ‘Responsible Use of Antimicrobials’ training to over 11,000 farm business members;Investing in innovation through the funding of a series of projects including the ‘Strategic Action on Antimicrobial Usage’ (STATUS) and Whole Genome Sequencing projects, the findings from which will be used to inform future formulation of policy and action on AMR.The UK Veterinary Antibiotic Resistance and Sales Surveillance Report (VARSS) 2020, UK Veterinary Antibiotic Resistance and Sales Surveillance Report (publishing.service.gov.uk) provides details of the sales of veterinary antibiotics for use in food producing animals across the UK over the past three years.  There has been a 50% reduction in the amount of active antibiotic ingredient sold between 2014 and 2020 (446 tonnes in 2014, 269 tonnes in 2020) with a 8% decrease reported between 2017 and 2020.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs
